entities.name: SEASONAL MIGRATION
entities.description: Temporary or seasonal movement of rural households to cities as a strategy to cope with shocks and generate income

entities.name: POVERTY
entities.description: A state of economic deprivation characterized by income below a specific poverty line

entities.name: DROUGHT
entities.description: A natural shock characterized by persistent lack of rainfall affecting rural households

entities.name: EMPLOYMENT
entities.description: Engagement in economic activities for income generation during lean seasons

entities.name: ASSET ACCUMULATION
entities.description: The process of acquiring assets over time as a coping mechanism and path out of poverty

entities.name: POVERTY REDUCTION
entities.description: The process of moving households from a poor to a non-poor category

entities.name: DOKUR VILLAGE
entities.description: A drought-prone village in Mahbubnagar District, Telangana, India

entities.name: MAHBUBNAGAR DISTRICT
entities.description: A district in Telangana State, India

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: The country where the rural households and the study are located

entities.name: VLS-VDS DATASET
entities.description: A longitudinal dataset from Village Level Studies and Village Dynamics Studies spanning 1975 to 2012

entities.name: PROBIT ANALYSIS
entities.description: A statistical method used to identify factors responsible for the decision to migrate

entities.name: POVERTY LINE
entities.description: A monetary threshold used to categorize households as poor or non-poor
